messages.content: Just got the R&R back from Genome Biology and honestly I'm kind of shocked - it's only minor revisions! After that Nature Methods disaster in December I was bracing for another round of major changes
That's fantastic news! What did the reviewers focus on for the minor revisions?

The best part is that reviewer 2 actually praised our statistical reanalysis - you know, the complete overhaul we did after that brutal NatMeth rejection. They said the bootstrapping approach was 'rigorous and well-executed' which is like... the complete opposite of what we heard before
What a turnaround! It sounds like pivoting to GB and strengthening the statistical framework really paid off. What are the specific revisions they're asking for?

Mostly just clarifying some methods details and adding one supplementary figure. Nothing that should take more than a week or two. Anika's already started on the revisions since she knows that analysis inside and out
That timeline sounds very manageable. Are you planning to leverage this acceptance for any upcoming work?

Actually yes - I'm already thinking about the second paper from our R01 preliminary data. We have this whole dataset that didn't make it into the GB paper and with the grant funding secured, I want to get that analysis pipeline running in the lab. Could be another solid publication by end of year
Smart to capitalize on the momentum! Having the R01 funding gives you the resources to really dig into that additional dataset. What's the focus of the second paper going to be?

We're looking at the temporal dynamics aspect - how the expression patterns change over longer time scales. The GB paper was more about the spatial clustering but there's this whole time series component that we barely touched. Plus with the new rotation student joining the lab this month, I have the hands to actually tackle it properly

summary: Priya receives minor revisions from Genome Biology after their statistical reanalysis was praised by reviewers, a stark contrast to the earlier Nature Methods rejection. She's planning a second paper using R01 preliminary data with help from new lab members.
